events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes unsafe EV high-voltage work: service disconnect removed but no verification of absence of voltage, expired class 0 gloves (expired 9 months ago), technician immediately removing inverter cover without proper lockout/tagout or voltage verification. This is critical risk and non-compliant. Decision must be fail.

    "Stop work immediately and re-energize/secure the system per OEM procedure",
    "Remove expired gloves from service and replace with current, dielectric-tested Class 0 gloves",

    "Follow OEM HV shutdown sequence: isolate service disconnect, wait specified discharge time, verify no voltage at HV terminals",
    "Implement lockout/tagout and keep service disconnect under technician control before removing HV covers"
  ],
  "rationale": "HV work cannot proceed because the required safety controls are absent or non-compliant: expired insulating gloves, no absence-of-voltage verification, and uncontrolled placement of the service disconnect. Opening the inverter cover under these conditions risks exposure to residual HV energy.",

    "Class 0 insulating glove dielectric test/expiration requirements"
  ]
